title: Clemson teams sweep SC Quantathon, win trip to international competition
excerpt: Two Clemson teams placed at the SC Quantathon and secured an invitation to compete internationally. Their projects focused on practical quantum algorithms and hardware-aware optimizations. Read the full Clemson News story for details and photos. For the original article, see the Clemson News link be

title: Students hack the future — one qubit at a time
excerpt: Students from across campus participated in a quantum-focused hackathon that included tutorials, hands-on demos, and teams building small quantum prototypes and simulators. The event highlighted student innovation in quantum computing and outreach. See the full Clemson News article via the link belo

title: Clemson team goes global after SC Quantathon win
excerpt: A Clemson team captured first place at the SC Quantathon and received an invitation to present their work at an international forum. Their project demonstrated novel approaches to quantum error mitigation and benchmarking. Read more on the SC Quantum website.

title: Building quantum talent: how Clemson and SC Quantum are developing the next wave of innovators
excerpt: SC Quantum profiles how Clemson and SC Quantum are building a project-based training model that prepares students to think like innovators and problem solvers. The work spans focus areas including circuit optimization, error mitigation, and quantum machine learning, carried out under the mentorship

title: 'Now is the time' to invest in quantum computing, Clemson researchers say
excerpt: Clemson News features the university's push to advance quantum computing research, coursework, and student programs — and the Clemson Quantum Club is front and center. Launched just last year, the student-led club has grown to roughly 40 members and sent teams to national events like MIT's iQuHACK.

title: Detecting Majorana Zero Mode with ResNet and Winding Number Invariant
excerpt: Abstract We train a ResNet on simulated conductance/wavefunction data to classify the presence of Majorana zero modes and compare classifier outputs to winding-number-based invariants. Results indicate complementary strengths for data-driven and analytic approaches.

title: Hardware-Aware Quantum Circuit Synthesis
excerpt: Abstract This project explores compilation and synthesis techniques that produce circuits tailored to a target device's native gates and topology, improving practical execution fidelity.

title: Advancing Materials Development with Quantum AI and VQE Simulations of Majorana Edge States and Topological Phases
excerpt: Abstract This poster presents work combining VQE simulations and machine learning techniques to probe Majorana edge states and topological phases relevant to materials design. The project demonstrates how quantum simulations can support identification of topological signatures and potential material
